title: Transindividuation in AI Systems: Simondon and the Collective Dimension of Machine Learning
abstract_snippet: We extend Simondon's concept of transindividuation to analyze how machine learning systems participate in collective processes of meaning-making and knowledge production.

title: Epistemic Injustice in Algorithmic Decision-Making: A Framework for Audit
abstract_snippet: Applying Fricker's framework of epistemic injustice to algorithmic systems, this paper develops a practical audit methodology for identifying testimonial and hermeneutical injustice in automated decision pipelines.

title: The Evidence Turn in AI Ethics: From Principles to Provenance
abstract_snippet: We argue that AI ethics must undergo an 'evidence turn' in which ethical claims about AI systems are grounded in traceable evidence chains rather than abstract principles, and propose a provenance-based framework for this shift.
